situation: audit-2026-07-03-012 finding SCRIPTS-L01 (priority 3, pending): verify-pattern.sh L21 used `python3 -c "import json,sys; json.load(open('$CAL'))"` which interpolates the bash variable directly into Python source. If the project path ever contained a single quote or other shell-special character, this would either break syntax (benign) or allow shell injection (if attacker-controlled path). Same anti-pattern was already fixed in the heredoc block at L28-30 via sys.argv; GATE-0 check was the lone holdout.
action: Replace interpolated form with `python3 -c 'import json,sys; json.load(open(sys.argv[1]))' "$CAL"`, passing the path as argv[1]. Mirrors existing heredoc convention in same file. Smoke-tested against live calibration.json: all 4 gates pass.
outcome_evidence: First commit attempt pushed 800214e to auto/opt-scripts-shift-1783076032 (verified in worktree: L21 now uses sys.argv[1]). Re-commit after linter reverted main correctly blocked by cp-guard ('worktree 与 main HEAD 不同，跳过以避免覆盖') — confirms fix lives in worktree awaiting merge. Main unchanged; smoke test ran all 4 gates successfully against /home/admin/workspace/autonomous-studio/.claude/decisions/calibration.json.
next_suggestion: 派生 fix #2: SCRIPTS-L02 (studio-precheck.sh bare except → narrow exception types)
派生 fix #3: SCRIPTS-L03 (autonomous-loop.sh PROMPT single-quote fragility)
人工 review opt-scripts-shift-1783076032（含 SCRIPTS-M01 + SCRIPTS-L01 两个 fix），merge 后 derived_fixes SCRIPTS-M01/L01 status→merged

audit_findings.finding: $CAL bash-interpolated into python -c source string. Path containing ' or shell metacharacters breaks syntax; attacker-controlled path enables code injection.
audit_findings.remediation: Pass path via sys.argv: python3 -c 'import json,sys; json.load(open(sys.argv[1]))' "$CAL"
